content.full_text:
June 25, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission  
Post Office Box 807  
1100 "K" Street  
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

The City of Costa Mesa has an active community Redevelopment Agency and has progressed to the point where it has established a project area and has adopted a Redevelopment Plan (see §33000 et seq. Health and Safety Code).

One of the City Councilman who was elected for the first time at the March 15, 1976 Municipal election owns an apartment house which is within the Community Redevelopment Project Area. The property is fully developed, was purchased in May of 1974 prior to his election as a City Councilman, and the project area and the Redevelopment Plan were all established by the Agency and approved by the City Council prior to his election.

It is not likely that an amendment to the Redevelopment Plan will be made which would need the approval of the City Council (see §33360 et seq. Health and Safety Code), so that the question of whether or not he is entitled to vote will likely not arise.

I have tentatively advised him that unless some decision of the City Council becomes necessary which would physically affect his property as part of an amendment to a Redevelopment Plan, he will be entitled to vote. After reading your letter-opinion No. 75-005 to William L. Owen, City Attorney for the City of Davis, I have a feeling that perhaps the posture of the Commission may be somewhat different than mine on this particular problem.

I do not feel that the question posed in this letter is of sufficient statewide impact to require a formal opinion from the Fair Political Practices Commission. In speaking with the City Councilman I am of the opinion that he will be satisfied with your direction indicating that he either may or may vote on matters requiring City Council action on the already adopted Redevelopment Plan.

I might mention, incidently, that he has made a disclosure under §33130 and §33130.5 Health and Safety Code, in writing, which has been filed with our City Clerk as a City Councilman, even though he is not a member of the Redevelopment Agency.

Your early attention in this matter will be greatly appreciated.

Very truly yours,

Roy E. June  
City Attorney

REJ:kmb  
cc: Councilman McFarland
